name: Brand in Category Queries
description: Checks whether the brand name appears in web-search results for "best [category]" queries. Brand presence in category contexts is the foundation of generative engine visibility — if AI systems don't see the brand in category content, they can't recommend it.
assessment_method: Run WebSearch for category query. Check if brand name appears in any result title, description, or snippet.

criteria.pass: Brand name appears in WebSearch results for "best [category]" or "[category] tools/apps"
criteria.fail: Brand not mentioned in any category search results

fix_template: OFF-PAGE WORK — get listed on review sites (G2, Product Hunt, Capterra), create "best [category]" content on your own blog, earn mentions in industry listicles.
sources: GEO paper: brand presence in category contexts is the foundation of generative engine visibility. If AI systems don't see the brand in category content, they can't recommend it.
notes: Requires runtime web-search results for category queries. Source category header labels all GEO checks directional model judgment.

name: Knowledge Panel/Entity Card
description: Checks whether searching the brand name produces a knowledge panel or entity card. Knowledge panel presence indicates Google recognizes the brand as an entity, which correlates with AI system awareness.
assessment_method: WebSearch for brand name. Check for structured information beyond organic links.

criteria.pass: Searching brand name produces a knowledge panel, entity card, or structured brand information
criteria.fail: No entity recognition — just regular organic results

fix_template: OFF-PAGE WORK — build structured entity presence: Wikipedia article (if notable), Wikidata entry, Google Business Profile, consistent NAP across directories.
sources: Knowledge panel indicates Google recognizes the brand as an entity. Wikipedia/Wikidata presence strengthens this. Entity recognition by Google correlates with AI system awareness.
notes: Requires runtime web-search results for the brand name.

name: AI Description Matches Positioning
description: Compares how search results and snippets describe the brand against the brand's own positioning on its website. When sources disagree, AI-synthesized brand descriptions become inaccurate or generic.
assessment_method: Compare WebSearch brand descriptions against homepage/about page brand statement.

criteria.pass: How search results/snippets describe the brand aligns with the brand's own positioning on its website
criteria.warn: Partial alignment but some outdated or inaccurate framing
criteria.fail: Search results describe the brand differently from how it describes itself, or brand has no consistent self-description

fix_template: Create one canonical brand sentence used identically across: homepage, about page, Organization schema description, social profiles, directory listings.
sources: AI systems synthesize brand descriptions from multiple sources. If sources disagree, the AI description becomes inaccurate or generic.
notes: Requires runtime web-search results for the brand name.

name: No Outdated/Incorrect AI Info
description: Cross-references search-result descriptions of the brand against current site content to detect outdated or wrong information. Outdated info in search sources propagates into AI-generated brand descriptions.
assessment_method: Cross-reference search result descriptions with current site content.

criteria.pass: Search results contain current, accurate information about the brand
criteria.fail: Search results contain significantly wrong information (old pricing, discontinued features, wrong category)

fix_template: Update your own site content, then update third-party profiles, then request Google review of knowledge panel if applicable.
sources: Outdated info in search results means AI systems will generate outdated brand descriptions. Freshness of ALL sources (not just your site) matters.
notes: Requires runtime web-search results for the brand name.

name: Brand Sentiment Positive/Neutral
description: Assesses the overall sentiment of search results for the brand name. AI systems reflect the web's consensus — negative review dominance translates to negative AI descriptions.
assessment_method: WebSearch brand name + "reviews". Assess overall sentiment of results.

criteria.pass: Search results for brand name show positive or neutral sentiment (good reviews, positive press, favorable comparisons)
criteria.fail: Negative sentiment dominates (bad reviews, complaints, unfavorable comparisons)

fix_template: OFF-PAGE WORK — review management strategy, customer success outreach, address negative reviews professionally, build positive testimonial content.
sources: AI systems reflect the web's consensus about a brand. Negative review dominance in search results translates to negative AI descriptions.
notes: Requires runtime web-search results for brand + "reviews" queries.

name: Brand Recommended Over Competitors
description: Checks the brand's position and framing in category-query results relative to competitors. Brand positioning in category content directly influences AI recommendations.
assessment_method: WebSearch "best [category]" results. Check brand's position and framing.

criteria.pass: Brand appears in top results for category queries AND is positioned favorably (recommended, highly rated)
criteria.warn: Brand mentioned but not recommended or positioned neutrally
criteria.fail: Brand not mentioned, or mentioned unfavorably compared to competitors

fix_template: OFF-PAGE WORK — create "[Brand] vs [Competitor]" content on your blog, build use-case-specific landing pages, earn placement in category listicles.
sources: GEO: brand positioning in category content directly influences AI recommendations. Owned comparison content ("Brand vs Competitor") helps control narrative.
notes: Requires runtime web-search results for category queries.

name: Consistent Entity Data Across Sources
description: Cross-references brand name, description, and category across multiple search-result sources. Entity consistency across the web strengthens AI confidence; inconsistency causes AI to pick randomly or blend a wrong description.
assessment_method: Cross-reference brand information across multiple search result entries.

criteria.pass: Brand name, description, and category are consistent across 3+ search result sources
criteria.fail: Major inconsistencies — different name variants, conflicting descriptions, wrong categories

fix_template: Audit and update all third-party profiles to use identical brand name, description, and category. Start with highest-authority sources first.
sources: Entity consistency across the web strengthens AI's confidence in brand information. Inconsistency = AI picks randomly or generates a blended (often wrong) description.
notes: Requires runtime web-search results across multiple sources.

name: sameAs Links to Authoritative Profiles
description: Parses the Organization schema sameAs array and verifies each URL resolves to a real, active profile. Active, verified external profiles strengthen entity recognition across all AI systems.
assessment_method: Parse Organization schema sameAs. WebFetch each URL to verify it resolves.

criteria.pass: Organization schema has sameAs array with 2+ URLs that resolve to real, active profiles
criteria.warn: sameAs URLs present but some don't resolve or are inactive

fix_template: Add active social/professional profile URLs to Organization schema sameAs array
sources: sameAs is the structured data mechanism for entity disambiguation. Active, verified external profiles strengthen entity recognition across all AI systems.
notes: Verification step requires fetching external profile URLs (outbound fetches beyond the audited page).
